data.title: EpicQuest Education falls on receipt of Nasdaq delinquency notification letter
data.description:
• EpicQuest Education Group (NASDAQ:EEIQ) said on Friday that it received a delinquency notification letter from Nasdaq on March 5, 2025.
  • The letter indicated that the company is not currently in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement set forth in Nasdaq's Listing Rules as the closing bid price for the company's common shares was below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days.
  • The letter provides that the company has a period of 180 calendar days from the date of the letter, or until September 1, 2025, to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • EEIQ -4.23% after hours to $0.7719.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Valaris files to sell 4.76M common shares by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Valaris (NYSE:VAL) filed a prospectus related to the offer and resale of up to about 15 million shares of common stocks.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: DexCom falls after warning letter from FDA related to quality concerns
data.description:
DexCom said that on Tuesday it received a warning letter from the U.S. FDA following inspections at its facilities in San Diego, California, and Mesa, Arizona related to concerns about manufacturing processes and quality management systems.

The FDA cited issues with DexCom’s response to prior inspection findings, known as Form 483 observations. The inspections took place at the San Diego facility between October 21 and November 7, 2024, and at the Mesa facility from June 10 to June 14, 2024.

DexCom said that it does not anticipate that the warning letter will significantly affect its manufacturing operations or its 2025 revenue forecast.

Despite the warning, the FDA has not placed any restrictions on DexCom’s ability to manufacture, market, or distribute its products. The  letter also does not require any product recalls or impact the company’s ability to seek FDA clearance for new products.

DexCom shares down about 8% after the bell.

data.title: Blueberries Medical announces C$1 million private placement
data.description:
• Blueberries Medical (OTCQB:BBRRF) on Friday announced a non-brokered private placement consisting of the sale of 96.06 million common shares at C$0.011 per common share for aggregate gross proceeds of approximately C$1.05 million.
  • The net proceeds from the sale of the common shares are expected to fund and expand operations, for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about March 25, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cognizant Technology gains on report of activist Mantle Ridge stake
data.description:
Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH) rose 5.5% in after hours trading after a report that activist investor Mantle Ridge has accumulated a more than $1 billion stake.

Mantle Ridge has been privately talking with Cognizant (CTHS) management about ways for the tech-services firm to increase its share price, according to a WSJ report  on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The activist first started purchasing shares in the second half of 2022, when the stock was trading in the high $50s to low $60s. 

Mantle Ridge doesn't plan to start a proxy fight to add directors at this time, according to the report. 

Cognizant Technology (NASDAQ:CTSH) is scheduled to host an investor day on March 25.

data.title: Golden Heaven files to sell class A 70 million ordinary shares by selling shareholders

  • Filing

data.title: The Arena Group appoints Paul Edmondson as its CEO
data.description:
• The Arena Group (NYSE:AREN) said on Friday that it has appointed Paul Edmondson as Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately.
  • Edmondson had been serving as interim CEO since February 12, 2025.Prior to that, Edmondson had been the company’s COO and President of Platform.
  • Edmondson began working with The Arena Group in 2018, when it acquired the business he founded and led, HubPages. He then served as COO from August 2018 until October 2019 when he was named the company’s President.
  • Before founding HubPages, Edmondson worked for Microsoft Corporation’s MSN Entertainment and developed applications for Hewlett-Packard Company.

data.title: Crypto exchange Kraken preparing for IPO next year - Bloomberg
data.description:
Crypto exchange Kraken is preparing to go public next year amid a more relaxed regulatory environment under the Trump administration.

Kraken, whose revenue more than doubled to $1.5 billion last year, is seeking an initial public offering as soon as Q1 of next year, according to a Bloomberg report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The timeline could still change.

In a sign of friendlier times, the U.S. Securities and Exchange staff agreed on Monday to dismiss its lawsuit against Kraken.  The SEC also recently dropped lawsuits and investigations against Coinbase Global (COIN), Robinhood Markets (HOOD, NFT marketplace OpenSea, Consensys, and Uniswap, recently. 

Other crypto-focused firms that are evaluating IPOs include Circle, BitGo, and crypto exchanges Bullish and Gemini.

data.title: Jennifer Garner's Once Upon a Farm considering IPO
data.description:
American actress Jennifer Garner’s organic baby food company could be the next celebrity-owned baby-centric publicly-traded company, following in the footsteps of Jessica Alba’s Honest Company (NASDAQ:HNST).

According to Bloomberg, Garner’s Once Upon a Farm is working with banks and could offer shares in an initial public offering later this year.

The company, which offers a subscription service as well as individual “bundles” of organic baby food pouches, was founded in Berkeley, California in 2015 by Cassandra Curtis and Ari Raz, later joining with former Annie’s CEO John Foraker and Garner. The company soon went on to increase production and expand distribution to more than 13,000 stores nationwide.

Thanks to its focus on organic baby food and with the help of Garner’s association, the company has gone on to make over $100M in revenue (as of 2023).

In May 2021, Alba’s The Honest Company (NASDAQ:HNST) raised $412.8M through a public offering price of $16 per share in May 2021. The stock went on to trade as high as $23.88 before falling as low as $3 per share a year later. HNST currently trades at $5 per share.

data.title: Walker & Dunlop announces pricing of amended credit agreement
data.description:
• Walker & Dunlop (NYSE:WD) said on Friday that it has priced its previously announced amended and restated senior secured credit agreement.
  • The $450M term loan facility under the amended and restated credit agreement will initially bear interest at a rate equal to SOFR plus 2.00%.
  • Following the first full fiscal quarter ending after the closing date, the applicable interest margin on the term loan facility will be subject to a 25 basis points step down if the company’s total leverage ratio is equal to or less than 2.00 to 1.00, as defined in the credit agreement.
  • The amended and restated credit agreement will also include a three-year $50M revolving credit facility that will bear interest at a rate equal to SOFR plus 1.75%.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from its previously announced offering of $400M aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes due 2033 and for general corporate purposes.
